summary: Audience‑recorded Warfield show with warm acoustic sets and a killer electric second half; ambience shines despite minor fade‑outs.
review: The tape captures the intimate Warfield atmosphere perfectly, letting listeners feel like they’re on the balcony beside Bob and hearing the first Jerry solo break.  Highlights include the mellow triple of “He’s Gone → The Wheel → Stella,” a soaring “Loser,” and a powerful “Miracle,” while the acoustic set’s Heaven‑Help jams and El Paso→Ripple add extra sparkle.  Sound quality is generally clear and full, though there’s a brief fade‑out on “Cold Rain & Snow” and one reviewer called it the worst recording they’ve heard.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating across ten reviews is 3.7; most reviewers praise the performance and ambience, while a few note serious recording flaws, giving an overall good‑to‑great impression.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear balcony‑feel capture with excellent room ambience; minor issues include a fade‑out on “Cold Rain & Snow” and occasional muffled sections noted by one reviewer.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic first set (Sept/Oct‑80 style) sets a mellow tone, leading into an energetic electric second and third set that builds momentum with strong song pairings.

band_member_comments.Jerry: First solo break in the second set is highlighted; his playing adds excitement.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned only in a request for a Box of Rain box set, no performance comment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Listeners feel like they’re sitting right next to him; praised for presence on tape.
